RU.note: Adopted by the RSFSR; the Julian calendar is still used by the Russian Orthodox Church.

RO.note: Adopted after the formation of Greater Romania.

GR.note: Adopted for civil purposes; the 'Revised Julian' calendar was adopted by the Church later in 1924.

TR.note: Adopted as part of Atatürk's Westernizing reforms, replacing the Rumi calendar.

CN.note: Officially adopted following the Xinhai Revolution, though lunisolar practice continued.

EG.note: Adopted for civil and government use by Khedive Ismail.

RS.note: Adopted by the Kingdom of Serbs, Croats and Slovenes (Yugoslavia).

EE.note: Transitioned alongside Russia during the independence period.

LV.note: Adopted during the German occupation/independence struggle.

LT.note: Adopted during the German occupation/independence struggle.

AL.note: Adopted upon declaration of independence from the Ottoman Empire.
